articles.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
articles.summary: The agreement to pause the fighting for two weeks came hours after President Trump threatened to destroy Iran’s “whole civilization.” Much remains uncertain.

articles.title: Even as They Praise Iran Cease-Fire, World Leaders Are Whipsawed by Trump
articles.summary: Across Europe and the globe, the war has damaged economies, roiled politics and underscored a lack of options in dealing with President Trump’s whims.

articles.title: Trump Backs Down on Iran, and an A.I. ‘Reckoning’
articles.summary: Plus, the great pistachio gamble.

articles.title: Georgia, Wisconsin Elections Show Declining Appetite for Republican Candidates
articles.summary: A Republican won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s seat, but Democrats shifted the district 25 points to the left since the 2024 presidential race. Conservative candidates lost in Wisconsin, too.

articles.title: Stung by Voters, Republican Legislators Move to Curb Citizen Initiatives
articles.summary: After citizens in Republican states used ballot measures to protect abortion, expand Medicaid and raise the minimum wage, statehouses are moving to make such initiatives much harder.

articles.title: Your sarcasm is showing — and its history is surprisingly violent
articles.summary: Some people use sarcasm jokingly. But funnily enough, we tend not to find it witty when we're on the receiving end.

articles.title: Colleges are trying to boost student voting. A Trump probe freezes data for that work
articles.summary: To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.

articles.title: Abortion clinics are closing nationwide. Could urgent care help fill the gap?
articles.summary: When the only clinic that offered abortions in Michigan's rural Upper Peninsula closed, an urgent care decided to step in to fill the gap. Now, others are considering similar moves as brick-and-mortar

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: U.S. and Iran agree to two week ceasefire, how Iranians are responding to the ceasefire, the effects of the war in Iran give investors around the world whiplash.

articles.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy
articles.summary: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.

articles.title: Oil Prices Plunge and Stocks Surge After Cease-Fire Deal
articles.summary: Markets reacted positively after the United States and Iran reached a last-minute cease-fire agreement.

articles.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire
articles.summary: As part of the agreement, set to take effect immediately, Trump said the U.S. and Israel would suspend bombing Iran for two weeks, subject to Iran following through on its commitment to reopen the Str

articles.title: In Georgia’s 14th District Special Runoff, Democrats Pulled Off Their Biggest Shift Yet
articles.summary: Shawn Harris lost by about 12 percentage points in the 14th Congressional District, but he shifted the district 25 points to the left.

articles.title: Telehealth abortion will remain available for now, after a federal judge's ruling
articles.summary: The abortion pill mifepristone must undergo a safety review by the FDA, the judge said. Louisiana's case seeking to ban its use through telemedicine will proceed after that review.

articles.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
articles.summary: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.

articles.title: The Artemis II crew saw parts of the moon never seen before. Here's what they said
articles.summary: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.

articles.title: A Moscow poet highlights the challenges of speaking out against the war in Ukraine
articles.summary: In Russia, protesting against the war in Ukraine can be dangerous. But one Russian poet living in Moscow, Vadim Dzyuba, is speaking out.

articles.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
articles.summary: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

articles.title: The Best Restaurants in Boston

articles.title: Australia charges ex‑soldier with 5 war‑crime murders in Afghanistan
articles.summary: Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.

articles.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
articles.summary: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
